title: Personal Loan Overview
content: Our personal loan offers flexible financing from ₹50,000 to ₹25,00,000 with competitive interest rates starting at 10.49% per annum. Tenure ranges from 12 to 60 months. No collateral required. Processing fee is 1-2% of the loan amount. Disbursement within 48 hours of approval for existing customers.

title: Home Loan Overview
content: Home loans available from ₹5,00,000 to ₹5,00,00,000 with interest rates starting at 8.35% per annum. Tenure up to 30 years. Loan-to-value ratio up to 80% for properties up to ₹30 lakhs and 75% for higher value properties. Balance transfer facility available from other lenders with no additional charges.

title: Late Payment Penalty Structure
content: If the EMI payment is not received by the due date, a late payment fee of 2% of the overdue EMI amount or ₹500 (whichever is higher) will be charged. The penalty is applied after a 3-day grace period from the due date. After 90 days of continuous non-payment, the account is classified as NPA (Non-Performing Asset) under RBI guidelines, which significantly impacts the borrower's credit score (CIBIL).

title: Grace Period Policy
content: A grace period of 3 calendar days is provided after the EMI due date. No late payment fee is charged if the payment is received within this grace period. However, this grace period does not extend to the credit bureau reporting date. Payments received after the 30th day are reported as 'overdue' to CIBIL.

title: Prepayment and Foreclosure Policy
content: For floating rate loans: No prepayment or foreclosure charges for individual borrowers as per RBI guidelines. For fixed rate loans: Foreclosure charges of 2% on the outstanding principal. Partial prepayment allowed in multiples of one EMI. Minimum 6 EMIs must be paid before prepayment is allowed.

title: Available Payment Methods for EMI
content: EMI payments can be made through the following methods: 1) Auto-debit (NACH/ECS) from registered bank account — recommended for timely payments. 2) Online payment via our website or mobile app using net banking, UPI, or debit card. 3) NEFT/RTGS transfer to our collection account (details on your loan statement). 4) Post-dated cheques (PDCs) submitted at branch. 5) Cash payment at any branch (receipt will be issued).

title: How to Access Your Loan Account Online
content: You can access your loan account details through: 1) Customer portal at portal.example-lending.com — view statements, download certificates, and track payments. 2) Mobile app available on Google Play and App Store. 3) Missed call service: Give a missed call to 1800-XXX-XXXX to get your outstanding balance via SMS. 4) WhatsApp banking: Send 'BALANCE' to our WhatsApp number for instant account details.

title: Handling Financial Difficulty Objection
content: When a borrower expresses financial difficulty, respond with empathy: 'I understand you're going through a challenging time. We have several options that may help.' Available options include: 1) EMI Holiday — pause payments for up to 3 months (eligible after 12 months of regular payment). 2) Loan Restructuring — extend tenure to reduce monthly EMI. 3) Payment Extension — extend the due date by up to 15 days without penalty. 4) Partial Payment — make a reduced payment with the remainder added to next month. Always check eligibility before offering these options.

title: Handling Charge Dispute Objection
content: When a borrower disputes charges: 'I understand your concern about the charges. Let me look into this for you.' Steps: 1) Verify the specific charge they're disputing. 2) Explain the charge based on the loan agreement terms. 3) If the charge appears incorrect, offer to raise a dispute ticket with resolution in 7 working days. 4) If the charge is correct but the borrower is dissatisfied, offer to connect with a senior team member.

title: Handling Already Paid Objection
content: When a borrower says they've already paid: 'Thank you for letting me know. Sometimes payments take 1-2 business days to reflect in our system.' Steps: 1) Ask for the payment date, amount, and method. 2) Check the system for recent payments. 3) If payment is found, confirm and apologize for the inconvenience. 4) If not found, request a payment receipt or screenshot for manual verification. 5) Assure them no late fee will be charged if payment was made before the due date.

title: RBI Fair Practice Code for Lending
content: Under RBI's Fair Practice Code, lenders must: 1) Clearly communicate all terms and conditions at the time of sanction. 2) Not charge foreclosure/prepayment penalties on floating rate term loans to individual borrowers. 3) Provide at least 30 days notice before changing interest rates. 4) Not use coercive methods for loan recovery. 5) Provide a detailed reason if a loan application is rejected. 6) Display the Grievance Redressal Officer's details prominently.

title: Required Call Disclosures
content: At the start of every pre-due reminder call, agents must: 1) Identify themselves by name and company. 2) State the purpose of the call (payment reminder). 3) Verify the identity of the person they are speaking with before sharing any loan details. 4) Mention that the call may be recorded for quality and training purposes. These disclosures are mandatory under the company's compliance policy and RBI guidelines.

title: Hardship Program Details
content: The Financial Hardship Program is available for borrowers facing temporary financial difficulty due to job loss, medical emergency, or natural disaster. Eligibility: Minimum 6 months of loan tenure completed and at least 50% payments made on time. Benefits: 1) EMI reduction of up to 50% for 3-6 months. 2) No late payment fees during hardship period. 3) No adverse credit bureau reporting. Application requires supporting documentation (medical bills, termination letter, etc.).

title: Loan Restructuring Options
content: Loan restructuring allows modification of loan terms to make payments more manageable: 1) Tenure Extension — extend the loan period by up to 24 months to reduce monthly EMI (e.g., ₹15,000 EMI becomes ₹12,500 with 12-month extension). 2) Step-up EMI — start with lower EMIs that gradually increase. 3) Interest Rate Renegotiation — available for customers with 24+ months of regular payment history. Processing fee of ₹500 applies for restructuring requests.

title: When to Escalate to Human Agent
content: Escalate the call to a human agent in the following situations: 1) Customer explicitly requests to speak with a human agent — always honor this request. 2) Identity verification fails after 3 attempts. 3) Customer expresses strong dissatisfaction or threatens legal action. 4) Customer reports fraud or unauthorized transactions on their account. 5) Technical issues prevent accessing the customer's account. 6) Customer has a complaint that requires investigation. 7) Customer asks about topics outside the agent's scope (investments, insurance, credit cards).
